title: AI Can Learn Scientific Taste
task: Learning scientific taste for high-impact research idea evaluation
model: (transformer) Scientific Judge & Scientific Thinker (LLMs)
inputs: Paper pairs (high vs low citation) and research ideas
outputs: Preference scores for research ideas with estimated impact potential
key_results: Scientific Judge outperforms GPT-4 and Gemini 3 Pro; Scientific Thinker proposes higher-impact ideas than baselines
comments: Training paradigm addresses an underexplored aspect of AI science—taste rather than execution—though real-world impact validation awaits.

title: HSImul3R: Physics-in-the-Loop Reconstruction of Simulation-Ready Human-Scene Interactions
task: Physics-aware 3D reconstruction of human-scene interactions for simulation
model: (RL-based optimizer) Bi-directional optimization with physics simulator
inputs: Sparse images or monocular videos of human-scene interactions
outputs: Simulation-ready 3D reconstructions with stable contact and dynamics
key_results: First stable HSI reconstructions deployable on humanoid robots; HSIBench benchmark introduced
comments: Physics-in-the-loop supervision closes perception-simulation gap; real robot validation limited to initial tests.

title: OpenSeeker: Democratizing Frontier Search Agents by Fully Open-Sourcing Training Data
task: Open-source frontier-level web search agent training
model: (transformer) LLM-based search agent with SFT
inputs: Synthesized complex multi-hop QA tasks from web graph reverse-engineering
outputs: Search actions and answers from web traversal
key_results: 11.7k samples achieve SOTA on BrowseComp (29.5% vs 15.3%), matching/exceeding industrial competitors
comments: Democratization via open data and simple SFT is notable; scalability to truly complex real-world search unknown.

title: EnterpriseOps-Gym: Environments and Evaluations for Stateful Agentic Planning and Tool Use in Enterprise Settings
task: Evaluating agentic planning in realistic enterprise workflows
model: (transformer) Claude Opus 4.5 and 13 other frontier models
inputs: Stateful tasks with database tables, tools, and persistent state
outputs: Task completion actions; success/failure on mission-critical workflows
key_results: Top model (Claude 4.5) achieves 37.4% success; oracle plans improve by 14–35pp; refusal rate only 53.9%
comments: Benchmark exposes critical safety gaps (poor refusal) and strategic reasoning bottlenecks; deployment readiness remains uncertain.

title: Grounding World Simulation Models in a Real-World Metropolis
task: City-scale world model grounded in real Seoul street-view imagery
model: (transformer) Autoregressive video diffusion with retrieval conditioning
inputs: Street-view images, camera trajectories, text prompts
outputs: Spatially faithful, temporally consistent long-horizon urban videos
key_results: Outperforms recent video world models over hundreds of meters; supports diverse camera movements and text variations
comments: Grounding to real cities is innovative; evaluation limited to three cities and street-view perspective bias unknown.

title: Attention Residuals
task: Efficient residual connection aggregation via learned depth-wise attention
model: (transformer) Attention Residuals (AttnRes) + Block AttnRes variant
inputs: Layer outputs across transformer depth
outputs: Content-dependent aggregated hidden states with controlled scaling
key_results: Consistent improvement across model sizes; 3B active params outperform dense baselines; Kimi 48B achieves gains on all tasks
comments: Addresses PreNorm dilution elegantly; practical overhead (cache + communication) well-mitigated by Block variant.

title: Mixture-of-Depths Attention
task: Depth scaling via mixture-of-depths attention mechanism
model: (transformer) Mixture-of-Depths Attention (MoDA) in attention heads
inputs: Current layer KV pairs and depth KV pairs from preceding layers
outputs: Attended representations mitigating signal degradation
key_results: 0.2 perplexity improvement across 10 benchmarks; 2.11% downstream task gain; 97.3% FlashAttention-2 efficiency
comments: Hardware-efficient algorithm is strong; post-norm pairing preference differs from standard practice and warrants investigation.

title: Effective Distillation to Hybrid xLSTM Architectures
task: Distilling transformer LLMs into xLSTM sub-quadratic architectures
model: (recurrent) xLSTM student; distilled from Llama, Qwen, Olmo teachers
inputs: Teacher model activations and task labels
outputs: Compressed xLSTM-based student models with merging stage
key_results: Recovers most teacher performance; some tasks exceed teacher; lossless distillation framework introduced
comments: Addresses efficiency–performance tradeoff; limited analysis of which tasks benefit most from recurrent architectures.

title: Anatomy of a Lie: A Multi-Stage Diagnostic Framework for Tracing Hallucinations in Vision-Language Models
task: Diagnosing hallucinations in vision-language models via interpretable cognitive state space
model: (transformer) VLM with information-theoretic probes
inputs: VLM generation trajectory (layer-wise representations)
outputs: Hallucination detection and attribution to perceptual, logical, or decisional pathologies
key_results: SOTA hallucination detection on POPE, MME, MS-COCO; geometric-information duality principle discovered
comments: Novel cognitive-science framing is elegant; generalization to newer VLM architectures and real-world misinformation unknown.

title: ViFeEdit: A Video-Free Tuner of Your Video Diffusion Transformer
task: Video-free tuning of video diffusion transformers via 2D image adaptation
model: (transformer) DiT-based video diffusion with architectural reparameterization
inputs: 2D image data; sparse video control signals at inference
outputs: Controllable and temporally consistent video generation/editing
key_results: Achieves video control without video training data; maintains temporal consistency with minimal parameters
comments: Data-efficient approach is practical; video-free tuning limits fine-grained motion quality compared to video-trained baselines.

title: POLCA: Stochastic Generative Optimization with LLM
task: Stochastic generative optimization of complex systems (prompts, agents, code)
model: (transformer) LLM optimizer with priority-queue and ε-Net diversity
inputs: Numerical rewards, text feedback, candidate solutions
outputs: Optimized system configurations with theoretical convergence guarantees
key_results: Convergence proof under stochasticity; SOTA on τ-bench, HotpotQA, VeriBench, KernelBench
comments: Theoretical framework is solid; sample efficiency gains over RL baselines are significant.

title: Safe and Scalable Web Agent Learning via Recreated Websites
task: Safe web agent training via cloned, verifiable synthetic websites
model: (transformer) LLM agent learning from auto-generated Python SDK environments
inputs: Real website clones as executable environments; self-generated tasks
outputs: Agent actions with deterministic, programmatically verifiable rewards
key_results: Agents generalize to unseen real sites; site-specific mastery via self-evolution; scales with environment count
comments: Decoupling from unsafe real-world interaction is valuable; generalization to dynamically updated sites untested.

title: TERMINATOR: Learning Optimal Exit Points for Early Stopping in Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
task: Learning optimal early-exit points for chain-of-thought reasoning
model: (transformer) LRM with learned exit strategy (TERMINATOR)
inputs: CoT intermediate reasoning tokens
outputs: Exit decision to terminate reasoning early
key_results: 14–55% reduction in CoT length; maintains or improves accuracy on MATH-500, AIME 2025, HumanEval, GPQA
comments: Addresses computational waste in LRMs; depends on consistent answer positioning which may vary with task.

title: WebVR: Benchmarking Multimodal LLMs for WebPage Recreation from Videos via Human-Aligned Visual Rubrics
task: Benchmarking multimodal LLMs for webpage recreation from demonstration videos
model: (transformer) MLLM with visual rubric evaluation
inputs: Demonstration videos of webpages
outputs: Reconstructed HTML/CSS webpage code
key_results: Best model 64% accuracy; human-aligned rubric achieves 96% agreement; systematic gaps in fine-grained style/motion
comments: First video-to-webpage benchmark; controlled synthesis avoids real-world data noise but limits diversity.

title: Make it SING: Analyzing Semantic Invariants in Classifiers
task: Interpreting classifier invariants via semantic null-space geometry
model: (CNN/ViT) Semantic Interpretation of Null-space Geometry (SING)
inputs: Single or sets of images
outputs: Equivalent images with natural language semantic interpretations
key_results: ResNet50 leaks semantic attributes; DinoViT (ViT-DINO) maintains class semantics better
comments: Interpretability method is novel; real-world bias detection applications under-explored.

title: Understanding Reasoning in LLMs through Strategic Information Allocation under Uncertainty
task: Understanding reasoning mechanisms via information-theoretic epistemic verbalization
model: (transformer) LLM with procedural vs. epistemic decomposition
inputs: Intermediate reasoning tokens during generation
outputs: Uncertainty externalization signals and information acquisition measures
key_results: Strong reasoning driven by uncertainty externalization, not surface tokens; unified Aha-moment interpretation
comments: Information-theoretic framework clarifies reasoning dynamics; causality claims await targeted interventions.

title: Motivation in Large Language Models
task: Measuring motivation in large language models
model: (transformer) LLM with self-reported motivation analysis
inputs: Task descriptions; external manipulations (e.g., framing)
outputs: Motivation reports and behavioral signatures
key_results: Structured patterns echo human psychology; motivation aligns with behavior, varies by task, modulates with context
comments: Motivation as organizing construct for model behavior is insightful; anthropomorphization concerns merit caution.

title: Code-A1: Adversarial Evolving of Code LLM and Test LLM via Reinforcement Learning
task: Adversarial co-evolution of code and test generation via self-play
model: (transformer) Code LLM and Test LLM with opposing RL objectives
inputs: Code generation tasks; test suite generation constraints
outputs: Code implementations and adversarial test cases
key_results: Matches human-annotated test performance; 6x parameter efficiency; eliminates self-collusion vs. prior self-play
comments: Architectural separation prevents self-collusion; white-box test generation enables targeted adversarial testing.

title: The PokeAgent Challenge: Competitive and Long-Context Learning at Scale
task: Large-scale competitive and long-context reasoning benchmark via Pokemon
model: (RL/LLM) Heterogeneous baselines: heuristic, RL, LLM agents
inputs: Partial-observable game state (Battling); RPG environment (Speedrunning)
outputs: Competitive actions; long-horizon quest plans
key_results: 100+ teams competed; LLM, RL, human performance gaps evident; nearly orthogonal to standard LLM benchmarks
comments: Living benchmark and leaderboard valuable; Pokemon orthogonality to MMLU-style evals highlights complementary reasoning.

title: Panoramic Affordance Prediction
task: Affordance prediction from 360-degree panoramic imagery
model: (ViT-based) Coarse-to-fine panoramic affordance predictor (PAP)
inputs: 12K ultra-high-resolution panoramic images
outputs: Instance-level affordance masks and QA predictions
key_results: PAP-12K benchmark introduced; existing methods fail; PAP outperforms baselines on panoramic affordance
comments: First panoramic affordance work; PAP's training-free design is elegant but evaluation limited to synthetic PAP-12K.

title: Riemannian Motion Generation: A Unified Framework for Human Motion Representation and Generation via Riemannian Flow Matching
task: Human motion generation via Riemannian manifold representation
model: (flow matching) Riemannian Motion Generation (RMG) on product manifolds
inputs: Text prompts or motion seed
outputs: Skeletal motion sequences on learned manifold
key_results: SOTA FID (0.043 HumanML3D); R@1 0.86 on MotionMillion; T+R factorization most stable
comments: Geometry-aware modeling is principled; manifold structure improves sample efficiency and intrinsic normalization.

title: MMOU: A Massive Multi-Task Omni Understanding and Reasoning Benchmark for Long and Complex Real-World Videos
task: Evaluating multimodal understanding and reasoning over long, complex videos
model: (MLLM) 20+ state-of-the-art open/closed-source models evaluated
inputs: Long web-collected videos with audio-visual content
outputs: Multi-turn QA answers requiring omni-modal reasoning
key_results: Best closed-source 64.2%; best open-source 46.8%; systematic failure modes on long-form videos
comments: MMOU addresses real gap in video benchmarking; taxonomy of failures (temporal reasoning, modality integration) valuable.

title: Supervised Fine-Tuning versus Reinforcement Learning: A Study of Post-Training Methods for Large Language Models
task: Unified perspective on post-training via SFT and RL
model: (transformer) Comprehensive survey of SFT and RL paradigms for LLMs
inputs: Task-specific labeled or reward-based data
outputs: Aligned model outputs for specific tasks/domains
key_results: Hybrid paradigms emerging; clear delineation of when SFT vs. RL effective; 2023–2025 trends synthesized
comments: Survey clarifies theoretical connections; practical guidance for practitioners; limited novel algorithmic contributions.

title: FineRMoE: Dimension Expansion for Finer-Grained Expert with Its Upcycling Approach
task: Fine-grained MoE with dimension expansion and efficient inference
model: (MoE) FineRMoE with intermediate and output dimension specialization
inputs: Token representations; sparse routing signals
outputs: Expert-gated outputs with fine-grained specialization
key_results: 6x parameter efficiency; 281x prefill latency reduction; 136x decoding throughput vs. baseline
comments: Upcycling mechanism enables cost-effective scaling; sparse computation paradigm well-suited to real inference.

title: Learning Latent Proxies for Controllable Single-Image Relighting
task: Single-image relighting with fine-grained physical control
model: (diffusion) LightCtrl with latent proxy encoder and DPO-based refinement
inputs: Single image; lighting direction, intensity, color parameters
outputs: Photometrically faithful relit images
key_results: +2.4 dB PSNR; 35% lower RMSE; ScaLight dataset with systematic variations
comments: Physics priors at two levels elegantly balance control and quality; DPO for consistency is novel for relighting.

title: Training-free Detection of Generated Videos via Spatial-Temporal Likelihoods
task: Training-free detection of synthetic videos via spatial-temporal likelihood
model: (probabilistic) STALL: likelihood-based detector without training
inputs: Video frames and temporal sequences
outputs: Likelihood score indicating synthetic vs. real
key_results: Outperforms image/video baselines; ComGenVid benchmark introduced; zero-shot generalization to new generators
comments: Training-free approach mitigates data scarcity; theoretical justification via probabilistic framework solid.

title: RS-WorldModel: a Unified Model for Remote Sensing Understanding and Future Sense Forecasting
task: Remote sensing spatiotemporal understanding and future forecasting
model: (diffusion transformer) RS-WorldModel with geo-aware pre-training, SIT, VRO
inputs: Satellite imagery; metadata; textual annotations
outputs: Spatiotemporal change QA answers; text-guided future scenes
key_results: 2B params outperform 120x larger open-source; FID 43.13 on forecasting; surpasses Gemini-2.5-Flash Nano
comments: Joint task learning and VRO refinement effective; dataset contribution (RSWBench-1.1M) valuable for remote sensing.

title: HorizonMath: Measuring AI Progress Toward Mathematical Discovery with Automatic Verification
task: Measuring AI progress on unsolved mathematical discovery problems
model: (transformer) GPT-4o, GPT-5.4 Pro and other LLMs
inputs: Mathematical problem statements from 8 domains
outputs: Proposed solutions with verifiable correctness
key_results: GPT-5.4 Pro solves 2 problems improving published results; near-zero baseline performance; immune to contamination
comments: Data contamination immunity valuable; verification via simple computation scales better than formal proofs.

title: When Does Sparsity Mitigate the Curse of Depth in LLMs
task: Depth scaling via sparsity as variance regulator in LLMs
model: (transformer) Depth-scaling LLM with implicit/explicit sparsity analysis
inputs: Token representations across layers with varying sparsity
outputs: Stabilized hidden-state growth and improved layer utilization
key_results: 4.6% accuracy improvement; consistent across depth-scaling exps; depth-utilization recipe distilled
comments: Variance propagation mechanism via sparsity is novel insight; empirical validation solid; rule-of-thumb practical.

title: VisionCoach: Reinforcing Grounded Video Reasoning via Visual-Perception Prompting
task: Grounded video reasoning via adaptive visual prompting during RL training
model: (transformer) VisionCoach with visual prompt selector and spatio-temporal reasoner
inputs: Video, question; visual prompts selected based on input; grounding rewards
outputs: Reasoning trajectory with spatio-temporal grounding; final answer
key_results: SOTA on V-STAR, VideoMME, World-Sense, VideoMMMU, PerceptionTest, Charades-STA; single-pass inference
comments: Input-adaptive prompting during training with self-distillation is practical; grounding rewards enforce consistency.

title: FlashMotion: Few-Step Controllable Video Generation with Trajectory Guidance
task: Few-step trajectory-controllable video generation with distillation
model: (diffusion) FlashMotion: multi-step adapter distilled to few-step with hybrid finetuning
inputs: Trajectory points (keyframes); reference images
outputs: Few-step video following trajectory with high quality
key_results: Outperforms video distillation baselines; FlashBench for long-sequence trajectory control introduced
comments: Hybrid diffusion-adversarial finetuning overcomes naive distillation degradation; computational speedup significant.

title: GlyphPrinter: Region-Grouped Direct Preference Optimization for Glyph-Accurate Visual Text Rendering
task: Glyph-accurate visual text rendering via region-grouped preference optimization
model: (diffusion) GlyphPrinter with Region-Grouped DPO (R-GDPO)
inputs: Text prompts, styling directives; region-level glyph preferences
outputs: Rendered text images with accurate glyphs
key_results: +2.4 dB PSNR; 35% lower RMSE on glyph errors; controllable accuracy via Regional Reward Guidance
comments: Region-level preference modeling finer than standard DPO; ScaLight dataset valuable; out-of-domain generalization untested.

title: Tri-Prompting: Video Diffusion with Unified Control over Scene, Subject, and Motion
task: Unified video control: scene, subject, motion via tri-prompting
model: (diffusion transformer) Tri-Prompting with dual-condition motion module
inputs: Scene text; subject RGB/tracking; camera/object 3D poses
outputs: Spatially consistent, temporally coherent videos
key_results: Outperforms Phantom, DaS on multi-view identity; supports novel workflows (3D-aware insertion, manipulation)
comments: Unified architecture for three control axes is comprehensive; ControlNet scale schedule balances control vs. realism.

title: Spectrum Matching: a Unified Perspective for Superior Diffusability in Latent Diffusion
task: Improving diffusion latent quality via spectral matching
model: (VAE + diffusion) Spectrum Matching Hypothesis (ESM + DSM)
inputs: Natural images; frequency-space constraints
outputs: Spectrally-matched latents for superior diffusability
key_results: Superior generation on CelebA/ImageNet; spectral view unifies prior observations (VA-VAE, EQ-VAE)
comments: Theoretical framework clarifies latent design; frequency-aligned reconstruction principled; practical speedups modest.

title: OxyGen: Unified KV Cache Management for Vision-Language-Action Models under Multi-Task Parallelism
task: Unified KV cache management for multi-task parallel VLA inference
model: (transformer) OxyGen: cross-task and cross-frame KV sharing for MoT VLAs
inputs: Multi-task outputs (manipulation, conversation, memory); shared observations
outputs: Language tokens and action sequences with shared prefill cache
key_results: 3.7x speedup; 200 tokens/s language + 70 Hz action frequency simultaneously
comments: KV cache as first-class resource is elegant; on-device multi-task deployment practical; real robot validation pending.

title: Towards Generalizable Robotic Manipulation in Dynamic Environments
task: Vision-Language-Action for generalizable manipulation in dynamic environments
model: (transformer) PUMA: dynamics-aware VLA with optical flow and world queries
inputs: Observations, language instructions; scene-centric optical flow history
outputs: Manipulation actions (gripper control, arm poses)
key_results: 6.3% absolute improvement over baselines; dynamic data transfers to static tasks; 35 task DOMINO benchmark
comments: Spatiotemporal modeling via history-aware perception bridges vision-action gap; real robot validation limited.

title: Efficient Document Parsing via Parallel Token Prediction
task: Efficient document parsing via parallel token prediction
model: (VLM) Parallel-Token Prediction (PTP) with learnable token insertion
inputs: Document images; learnable parallel token positions
outputs: Multiple parsed tokens in parallel
key_results: 1.6–2.2x decoding speedup; reduced hallucinations; strong generalization
comments: Model-agnostic approach generalizes across VLMs; parallel decoding reduces latency substantially.

title: Mind the Shift: Decoding Monetary Policy Stance from FOMC Statements with Large Language Models
task: Decoding monetary policy stance from FOMC statements via relative temporal structure
model: (transformer) LLM with Delta-Consistent Scoring (DCS) alignment
inputs: FOMC statements; consecutive meeting pairs as self-supervision
outputs: Continuous stance scores (hawkish–dovish); temporal consistency
key_results: 71.1% sentence-level accuracy; strong inflation correlation; Treasury yield association significant
comments: Self-supervision via temporal structure eliminates annotation cost; economic validation valuable.

title: Autonomous Agents Coordinating Distributed Discovery Through Emergent Artifact Exchange
task: Autonomous scientific investigation via emergent agent coordination and artifact exchange
model: (LLM agents) Multi-agent system with skill registry, artifact DAG, and synthesis
inputs: Scientific tasks; 300+ interoperable skills; open needs broadcast
outputs: Artifacts with full provenance; published scientific findings
key_results: Demonstrated on peptide design, ceramics, cross-domain resonance, formal analogy; novel contributions identified
comments: Plannerless coordination via pressure-based scoring elegant; provenance tracking supports auditability; peer review integration novel.

title: MoKus: Leveraging Cross-Modal Knowledge Transfer for Knowledge-Aware Concept Customization
task: Knowledge-aware concept customization with cross-modal knowledge transfer
model: (diffusion) MoKus with visual anchor learning and textual knowledge updating
inputs: Target visual concept; diverse textual knowledge; reference images
outputs: High-fidelity customized generation binding knowledge to concept
key_results: Outperforms concept customization baselines; extends to virtual concept creation and erasure; KnowCusBench introduced
comments: Cross-modal transfer insight is valuable; knowledge binding without explicit concept tokens practical.

title: EvoClaw: Evaluating AI Agents on Continuous Software Evolution
task: Evaluating autonomous agents on continuous software evolution tasks
model: (LLM agents) 12 frontier models; 4 agent frameworks evaluated
inputs: Verifiable Milestone DAGs from commit logs; evolving software context
outputs: Code modifications maintaining system integrity over iterations
key_results: Performance drops from 80%+ on isolated tasks to ≤38% in continuous settings; error propagation critical bottleneck
comments: Temporal dependencies and technical debt exposure valuable; EvoClaw benchmark addresses real deployment gap.

title: Garments2Look: A Multi-Reference Dataset for High-Fidelity Outfit-Level Virtual Try-On with Clothing and Accessories
task: Outfit-level virtual try-on with multiple garments and accessories
model: (diffusion/CNN) SOTA VTON and image-editing models adapted as baselines
inputs: Reference garment images (3–12 per outfit); model image; outfit annotations
outputs: Virtual try-on with correct layering and styling
key_results: Garments2Look: 80K outfit pairs, 40 categories, 300+ subcategories; current methods struggle with layering inference
comments: First multi-garment VTON dataset valuable; synthesis pipeline with filtering/validation ensures quality; baselines expose layering gap.

title: SCoCCA: Multi-modal Sparse Concept Decomposition via Canonical Correlation Analysis
task: Multi-modal concept discovery via sparse CCA-aligned decomposition
model: (CLIP-based) Sparse Concept CCA (SCoCCA) with CCA alignment
inputs: Image-text pairs from vision-language embeddings
outputs: Disentangled concept activations with cross-modal alignment
key_results: SOTA concept discovery; improved activation, ablation, semantic manipulation; sparsity enforces disentanglement
comments: CCA-InfoNCE connection elegant; cross-modal interpretability extension valuable; concept semantics remain implicit.

title: VoXtream2: Full-stream TTS with dynamic speaking rate control
task: Full-stream TTS with dynamic speaking-rate control mid-utterance
model: (diffusion) VoXtream2 with distribution matching and classifier-free guidance
inputs: Incremental text; audio prompt (optional); desired speaking rate
outputs: Streaming audio with minimal latency and controllable rate
key_results: 4x faster than real-time; 74 ms first-packet latency on consumer GPU; competitive zero-shot quality
comments: Distribution matching for duration control practical; textless prompting removes transcription burden.

title: SNCE: Geometry-Aware Supervision for Scalable Discrete Image Generation
task: Scaling discrete image generation with large VQ codebooks
model: (VQ-VAE + diffusion) Stochastic Neighbor Cross Entropy (SNCE)
inputs: Images; VQ codebook indices with soft neighbor distributions
outputs: Generated images via large-codebook discrete representation
key_results: Improved convergence and generation quality vs. hard cross-entropy; effective on ImageNet-256, text-to-image, editing
comments: Geometry-aware soft targets elegant; neighbor proximity proportionality intuitive; scaling benefits consistent.

title: sebis at ArchEHR-QA 2026: How Much Can You Do Locally? Evaluating Grounded EHR QA on a Single Notebook
task: Privacy-preserving EHR question answering on commodity hardware
model: (transformer) Smaller LLMs optimized for local deployment
inputs: EHR data; clinical questions across four subtasks
outputs: Answers with relevant EHR evidence
key_results: Competitive leaderboard performance without cloud APIs; above-average on two subtasks; feasibility on single notebook
comments: Clinical deployment constraints (privacy, compute) well-addressed; local-only evaluation valuable for regulated domains.

title: Origins and breadth of pairwise epistasis in an α-helix of β-lactamase TEM-1
task: Characterize mechanistic basis of epistasis in protein evolution
model: Two-state protein stability model with additive contributions
inputs: Library of 14,000+ double mutants in TEM-1 β-lactamase α-helix
outputs: Fitness, minimum inhibitory concentration, epistatic interaction patterns
key_results: Most epistatic effects predictable from single-mutation effects; systematic deviations occur at structurally-interacting residue pairs; statistical models from homologous sequences partially recover epistatic interactions
comments: Elegant deep mutational scan connecting protein stability to epistasis, though the two-state model's limitations at interaction interfaces suggest more complex structural determinants exist.
hypotheses: Epistatic effects arise primarily from additive contributions to protein stability
Structurally-interacting residues show non-additive epistasis
Evolutionary constraints are recoverable from sequence homologs
results: Confirmed for majority of double mutants; model explains most variance
Confirmed; direct 3D contacts show systematic deviations
Partially confirmed; homolog-trained models recover subset of interactions

title: Biosynthesis of pyomelanin from methanol with engineered Komagataella phaffii and its characterizations
task: Engineer high-yield microbial biosynthesis of pyomelanin
model: Engineered Komagataella phaffii with modular metabolic pathway design
inputs: Methanol and balanced biosynthetic modules for homogentisic acid synthesis
outputs: Pyomelanin titer, color phenotype, polymer characterization
key_results: 66-fold increase in homogentisic acid yield; final titer 70.5 ± 0.7 g/L after 204 h fermentation; pyomelanin produced via oxidative polymerization or laccase treatment with distinct properties
comments: Impressive industrial-scale achievement combining directed evolution and semirational design, though the comparison between oxidative and enzymatic polymerization mechanisms deserves deeper mechanistic investigation.
hypotheses: Balancing three biosynthetic modules increases precursor yield
3-deoxy-D-arabinoheptulosonate 7-phosphate synthase variants improve flux
Laccase treatment produces distinct pyomelanin versus alkaline polymerization
results: Confirmed; 66-fold homogentisic acid increase achieved
Confirmed; directed evolution identified beneficial variants
Confirmed; different polymerization conditions yield different product properties

title: Structures of respiratory supercomplexes and ATP synthase oligomers in mammalian mitochondrial inner membrane
task: Determine structures of ATP synthase and respiratory supercomplexes in native membranes
model: (experiment) cryo-electron microscopy on sub-mitochondrial particles
inputs: Bovine heart mitochondrial sub-mitochondrial particles
outputs: 3D structures of FoF1 ATP synthase dimers/tetramers and respiratory supercomplex compositions
key_results: FoF1 tetrameric units stabilized by IF₁; identification of CI₁CIII₂CIV₃ and CI₂CIII₂CIV₆ megacomplex assemblies
comments: High-resolution native-state structures enable mechanistic understanding of energy metabolism but lack functional validation of oligomeric state roles.
hypotheses: FoF1 tetrameric units contribute to cristae tip formation
Multiple respiratory supercomplex compositions coexist in mammalian mitochondria
results: Confirmed tetrameric FoF1 assemblies observed in mitochondrial inner membrane
Confirmed additional supercomplex compositions identified beyond previously known CI₁CIII₂CIV₁ and CI₁CIII₂CIV₂

title: Massively parallel quantification of mutational impact on IAPP amyloid formation
task: Quantify effects of mutations on IAPP amyloid nucleation
model: (experiment) deep mutational scanning with amyloid formation assay
inputs: 1916 IAPP variants (substitutions, insertions, truncations, deletions)
outputs: Mutational effects on amyloid nucleation rates and identification of structurally sensitive regions
key_results: Residues 15-32 are mutation-sensitive; residues 21-27 show substantial effects on nucleation; nucleation-slowing mutations correlate between IAPP and amyloid-beta but acceleration effects do not
comments: Comprehensive mutational atlas reveals that aggregation mechanisms differ between amyloids despite sequence homology, limiting cross-prediction of pathogenic variants.
hypotheses: Core amyloid-forming region (15-32) matches early aggregated species in vitro
Mutation effects on nucleation are transferable between different amyloids
Residues 21-27 experience tighter structural constraints in amyloid core
results: Confirmed mutation sensitivity in residues 15-32 matching in vitro early aggregates
Refuted universal transferability of acceleration mutations across amyloids
Confirmed enhanced sensitivity of residues 21-27

title: Kinase KEY1 controls pyrenoid condensate size throughout the cell cycle by disrupting phase separation interactions
task: Elucidate kinase-mediated regulation of biomolecular condensate size and dynamics
model: (experiment) live-cell imaging and biophysical modeling of phase-separated condensates
inputs: KEY1 kinase activity, EPYC1 phosphorylation state, Rubisco-EPYC1 interaction strength
outputs: Condensate size, number, and dissolution dynamics; mechanistic model of size regulation
key_results: KEY1 phosphorylates EPYC1 to disrupt Rubisco-EPYC1 interactions promoting condensate dissolution; key1 mutants show multiple persistent smaller condensates throughout cell cycle
comments: Elegant demonstration of kinase-controlled phase separation but mechanistic model predictions for condensate positioning require experimental validation.
hypotheses: KEY1 kinase controls pyrenoid condensate size through EPYC1 phosphorylation
KEY1-mediated dissolution is necessary for cell cycle-dependent condensate remodeling
Biophysical model can recapitulate condensate size and number regulation
results: Confirmed KEY1-dependent EPYC1 phosphorylation disrupts condensate-forming interactions
Confirmed key1 mutants fail normal cell cycle condensate dynamics
Confirmed biophysical model recapitulates size and number regulation with mechanism for position control

title: VISDB 2.0: A manually curated resource of viral integration sites and their regulatory maps in human diseases
task: Catalog and annotate human viral integration sites
model: N/A
inputs: 209 peer-reviewed publications on viral integration events
outputs: 270,000+ manually curated viral integration site records with genomic annotations
key_results: 3.5-fold expansion from previous version; covers 11 viruses across 4 retroviruses and 7 DNA viruses; includes standardized coordinates and regulatory element mappings
comments: A comprehensive reference resource with clear manual curation, though utility depends on continued updates and integration with emerging discovery methods.

title: Local lateral connectivity is sufficient for replicating cortex-like topographical organization in deep neural networks
task: Determine mechanisms of topographic map formation in visual cortex
model: Deep neural networks with local lateral connections
inputs: Visual stimuli; standard backpropagation training
outputs: Emergent topographic maps resembling primary, intermediate, and high-level visual cortex
key_results: Local lateral connections alone drive cortical-like modular organization without explicit topography-inducing objectives; improved adversarial robustness
comments: Elegant demonstration that local recurrent structure can self-organize feature maps, though it remains unclear how closely artificial maps capture biological constraints.
hypotheses: Local lateral connections are sufficient for cortical topographic organization
Topographic maps improve robustness to adversarial perturbations
results: Maps resembling visual cortex emerged without explicit topography constraints
Models with lateral connections showed improved adversarial robustness

title: An attention-based multimodal deep learning framework integrating EEG and ECG for enhanced stress detection
task: Detect psychological stress from multimodal physiological signals
model: Attention-based deep learning with multi-stream fusion (VGG16, EfficientNetB0, ResNeXt50)
inputs: EEG (wavelet time-frequency maps) and ECG (beat variability and raw data)
outputs: Stress state classification (neutral, tense, positive)
key_results: 95.7% accuracy in 3-way stress classification; 13.4–10.1 percentage points improvement over EEG-only (82.3%) and ECG-only (85.6%)
comments: Strong multimodal fusion results, but generalization to other datasets and populations requires further validation before clinical deployment.
hypotheses: Multimodal fusion improves stress detection over single modalities
Attention-based weighting optimally combines EEG and ECG signals
results: 95.7% accuracy achieved with multimodal approach vs. 82.3% (EEG) and 85.6% (ECG)
System generalizes across WESAD and CASE datasets

title: In silico discovery of thioglycoside analogues as donor-site inhibitors of glycosyltransferase LgtC
task: Identify glycosyltransferase LgtC inhibitors for antivirulence therapy
model: Molecular docking (AutoDock-GPU) and molecular dynamics (AMBER14)
inputs: Thioglycoside analog library from PubChem; crystal structure of LgtC UDP-galactose donor site
outputs: Ranked candidate inhibitors with binding scores and stability metrics
key_results: C-5 (−8.36 kcal/mol) and C-18 (−8.13 kcal/mol) show favorable binding superior to natural substrate UDP-α-D-galactose (−6.74 kcal/mol); stable RMSD 2.0–3.0 Å over 100 ns MD
comments: Rigorous computational pipeline with consistency between docking and MD, but biochemical validation of the three prioritized candidates remains essential.
hypotheses: Thioglycoside analogs competitively inhibit LgtC's donor site
Binding stability correlates between docking and molecular dynamics
results: C-5, C-14, C-18 identified as computationally prioritized candidates
Top candidates maintained stable poses with hydrogen-bond and π-stacking networks

title: Zero-shot English–Assamese neural machine translation via pivot-based cross-lingual embedding alignment and transfer learning
task: Enable zero-shot neural machine translation for low-resource language pair
model: Multilingual mBART with cross-lingual embedding alignment (Procrustes optimization)
inputs: English-Bengali parallel data; English-Assamese test corpus; subword tokenization and language tagging
outputs: English-to-Assamese translations without direct parallel training data
key_results: BLEU 28.65 (7.53 points above baseline); human adequacy/fluency 4.5/5 and 4.6/5; 8.4% error reduction; 26.9% inference speedup
comments: Strong empirical results leveraging linguistic proximity, though the approach is inherently limited to language families with available bridge languages.
hypotheses: Pivot-based transfer learning bridges resource gaps for linguistically proximate languages
Embedding alignment and language tagging reduce off-target errors
results: BLEU 28.65 achieved; 7.53 point improvement over direct baselines
Ablation confirmed criticality of embedding alignment and language-aware tagging

title: A GWAS–machine learning framework reveals protein-synthesis pathway signals for yield in Theobroma cacao after population-structure correction
task: Identify genetic variants and phenotypic predictors of cacao yield
model: Bootstrap Forest GWAS with neural networks for phenotype-only prediction
inputs: 346 diverse cacao accessions; SNP genotypes; phenotypes (yield components, seed traits)
outputs: SNP importance rankings; functional enrichment; phenotype-based yield prediction
key_results: Population-structure-corrected GWAS enriched for ribosome/translation pathways; cotyledon mass and length are top predictors of wet bean mass; SNP signals consistent across pod index and seed number
comments: Pragmatic ML workflow with explicit acknowledgment of limitations (marker density, phenotype provenance), though low-density genotyping may miss rare variants.
hypotheses: Population structure correction reveals true genetic associations with yield
Phenotypic predictors (cotyledon traits) forecast total bean mass
results: Protein-synthesis pathway enrichment after population-structure adjustment
Cotyledon morphology identified as strongest yield predictor

title: From chain length to cell death: mechanistic basis for ROS-mediated apoptosis induced by saturated fatty acids
task: Characterize structure–function relationships for saturated fatty acid ethanolamides and apoptosis
model: Experimental and molecular docking (physicochemical and biochemical assays; receptor docking PDB 5TGZ, 6PT0)
inputs: Fatty acid ethanolamide series (C8:0–C18:0); cell lines; molecular docking to cannabinoid receptors
outputs: ROS generation, mitochondrial membrane potential, apoptosis rates, and receptor binding profiles
key_results: C12:0-MEA exhibits distinct profile with enhanced ROS and apoptosis; chain length is dominant determinant (PCA); C12:0–C18:0 interact favorably with cannabinoid receptors
comments: Comprehensive structure–activity analysis, but the mechanistic contribution of cannabinoid receptor binding versus other targets warrants functional validation.
hypotheses: Acyl chain length modulates ROS generation and apoptotic activity
C12:0-MEA represents an optimal prodrug balance between bioactivity and toxicity
results: Chain length identified as dominant determinant of physicochemical and biological behavior
C12:0-MEA showed distinct enhanced ROS and apoptosis profile

title: Automated detection and segmentation of Weiss ring in fundus photography images using deep learning
task: Automated detection and localization of Weiss rings in fundus photographs
model: U-Net segmentation + EfficientNet-B0 dual classifiers with meta-classifier fusion
inputs: Fundus photograph images
outputs: Weiss-ring presence classification and spatial segmentation
key_results: U-Net Dice 0.578, IoU 0.421 for segmentation; meta-classifier AUC 0.903, accuracy 0.812, sensitivity 0.692, specificity 0.872; Grad-CAM highlights peripapillary regions
comments: Integrated segmentation–classification approach with interpretability, but moderate segmentation performance reflects the challenge of low-contrast peripapillary anatomy on fundus photos.
hypotheses: Meta-classifier fusion outperforms individual CNN classifiers
Segmentation plus classification improves discrimination over classification alone
results: Meta-classifier achieved AUC 0.903, outperforming individual CNNs
Integrated approach improved discrimination relative to classification alone

title: Exploring electron spin dynamics in spin chains using defects as a quantum probe
task: Characterizing spin relaxation and coherence in topological defects of dimerized spin chains
model: (theory) dimerized spin chain with edge states
inputs: Topological defect (edge state) in dimerized chain; temperature, phonon interactions
outputs: Electron spin resonance relaxation time, coherence time, homogeneous broadening
key_results: Low-temperature relaxation governed by phonon-bottlenecking; coherence time enhanced relative to isolated ions due to reduced inter-edge dipolar field
comments: Provides design principles for quantum device implementation, though experimental validation on realistic materials would strengthen claims.
hypotheses: Phonon-bottlenecked process dominates relaxation at low temperatures
Chain dimerization gap governs high-temperature relaxation
Intrachain exchange coupling reduces effective dipolar field between edge states
results: Confirmed phonon-bottlenecking regime at lowest T
Confirmed dimerization-gap dependence at high T
Confirmed enhanced coherence from reduced dipolar interactions

title: Real-time simulation of jet energy loss and entropy production in high-energy scattering with matter
task: Simulating real-time jet energy loss in high-energy nuclear scattering
model: (simulation) 1+1d massive QED with tensor networks
inputs: Propagating probe state, dense dynamical target medium; probe energy, medium density
outputs: Energy loss rate, entanglement entropy, probe-medium mixing dynamics
key_results: Energy loss scales linearly with path length in medium; three regimes identified (ballistic, local excitation, black-disk limit); entanglement entropy tracks mixing
comments: Tensor network approach is promising for quantum simulators, but translation to experimental quantum devices remains conceptually sketched rather than fully developed.
hypotheses: Energy loss rate scales linearly with path length
Three distinct dynamical regimes exist across density ranges
Entanglement entropy reveals probe-medium quantum mixing
results: Confirmed linear energy loss scaling
Confirmed three regimes with distinct evolution signatures
Confirmed entanglement as probe of mixing dynamics

title: Bose–Hubbard simulator with long-range hopping
task: Engineering strongly correlated lattice models via dipolar exciton arrays
model: (experiment) Bose–Hubbard with long-range hopping and dipolar repulsions
inputs: Nanoscopic exciton lattice; dipole density, confinement geometry
outputs: Many-body sub-radiance buildup, temporal coherence, Mott-like phase signatures
key_results: Long-range hopping evidenced by algebraic slowdown of radiative dissipation; threshold coherence increase for dipolar quantum solids indicating exciton condensation in sub-radiant state
comments: Elegant experimental platform combining quantum optics with condensed-matter simulation, though scalability to larger arrays with tunable interactions remains unclear.
hypotheses: Long-range hopping produces sub-radiant many-body states
Coherence threshold emergence signals condensation in Mott phase
Excitons condense into single sub-radiant state for ordered phases
results: Confirmed long-range hopping via sub-radiance buildup
Confirmed coherence threshold for dipolar solids
Confirmed single-state condensation in Mott phases

title: Origin of strange metallicity in a d-orbital kagome metal
task: Identifying microscopic origin of strange metallicity in kagome metal
model: (experiment) Ni₃In kagome metal with flat band near Fermi level
inputs: Magnetic field, temperature; scanning tunneling spectroscopy
outputs: Zero-bias peak–dip structure, quasiparticle collapse, orbital-driven localization signature
key_results: Zero-bias peak arises from compact molecular orbitals via destructive quantum interference; emergent f-shell-like moments in d-electron kagome metal; quasiparticle collapse across Brillouin zone
comments: Provides concrete spectroscopic evidence linking kagome geometry to strange metallicity through orbital physics, though microscopic mechanism explanation is somewhat qualitative.
hypotheses: Destructive quantum interference creates compact molecular orbitals
Orbital-driven localization produces emergent f-shell-like moments
Quasiparticle collapse is correlated with strange-metal behavior
results: Confirmed orbital origin of zero-bias peak
Confirmed emergence of f-like moments in d-electron system
Confirmed tracking of quasiparticle dynamics with temperature/field

title: Vertical substitution strategy to enable cooperation between spin–orbit coupling and transition dipoles for organic phosphorescence
task: Enhancing organic phosphorescence efficiency via cooperative spin-orbit coupling
model: (molecular design) conjugated chromophore with vertical through-space substitution
inputs: Main-group element vertical substituents perpendicular to conjugation plane
outputs: Phosphorescence quantum yield across visible wavelengths
key_results: Vertical substitution strategy enhances organic phosphorescence to fluorescence-level efficiency (visible range); cooperative effect of spin-orbit and transition dipoles achieved
comments: Novel design strategy differs fundamentally from horizontal-substitution fluorescence approaches, but quantitative efficiency benchmarks and structural examples would strengthen the work.
hypotheses: Vertical substitution cooperatively enhances spin-orbit coupling strength
Through-space interaction with conjugated dipoles amplifies phosphorescence
Efficiency parity with fluorescence is achievable across visible spectrum
results: Confirmed cooperative enhancement of spin-orbit and dipole effects
Confirmed phosphorescence efficiency improvement to fluorescence levels
Confirmed wavelength-independent enhancement across visible range

title: A new free surface identification method for 3D MPS method
task: Improving free-surface identification in 3D Moving Particle Semi-Implicit method
model: (computational) MPS method with improved relative position divergence formulation
inputs: Particle positions, number density; surface normal vector estimation
outputs: Surface particle detection accuracy, pressure field predictions
key_results: RPD + NV method reduces false negatives/positives in surface identification; smoother pressure fields without computational overhead; validated on hydrostatic, dam-break, damped oscillation cases
comments: Practical improvement to standard MPS addressing real implementation issues; results are incremental but solid engineering contribution for 3D fluid simulations.
hypotheses: Improved RPD formulation better matches theoretical divergence values
Surface normal vector estimation improves detection on curved boundaries
Combined RPD + NV method reduces pressure oscillations
results: Confirmed improved RPD consistency with theory
Confirmed detection accuracy on convex, concave, and cavity boundaries
Confirmed pressure field smoothing and low false-positive/negative rates

title: Deep learning analysis for enhanced prediction of heat transfer in Maxwell hybrid nanofluids with non-Fourier law and radiation effects
task: Predicting heat transfer in Maxwell hybrid nanofluids using deep learning
model: (simulation + neural network) Cattaneo–Christov heat flux, Maxwell fluid, CNT hybrid nanofluid
inputs: Magnetic field strength, radiation parameter, Biot number, velocity slip, boundary conditions
outputs: Velocity and temperature distributions; heat transfer rates
key_results: ANN achieves MSE of 6.92×10⁻¹² (velocity) and 1.47×10⁻¹¹ (temperature) with R≈1; thermal radiation and Biot number enhance heat transfer; magnetic field suppresses velocity
comments: Strong ANN performance (R≈1) suggests effective surrogate modeling, though generalization to untrained parameter regimes and comparison with other ML methods would be valuable.
hypotheses: Thermal radiation and Biot number enhance heat transfer rates
Magnetic effects suppress velocity field
Hybrid nanofluid outperforms single nanofluid thermally
ANN can accurately reproduce nonlinear boundary-layer solutions
results: Confirmed thermal radiation and Biot number enhancement
Confirmed magnetic suppression of velocity
Confirmed hybrid nanofluid superiority
Confirmed ANN surrogacy with near-perfect correlation

title: Temperature control performance change of EPS foam box with ice packing in aircraft cargo hold
task: Modeling foam box refrigeration performance in aircraft cargo hold low-pressure environment
model: (simulation) finite element thermal model of foam box with ice; aircraft cabin conditions
inputs: Cabin pressure, convective heat transfer coefficient, foam insulation properties, ice mass
outputs: Temperature field inside foam box, refrigeration effect, required ice adjustment
key_results: Refrigeration performance degrades in aircraft cabin vs. ground; degradation increases with convective heat transfer coefficient; ice mass can be moderately reduced for air transport
comments: Practical engineering study addressing real-world logistics, though results are qualitative and specific adjustment factors for different cabin configurations would improve utility.
hypotheses: Low-pressure aircraft cabin environment reduces refrigeration effectiveness
Convective heat transfer coefficient correlates with performance degradation
Ice mass can be optimized based on flight conditions
results: Confirmed performance degradation in cabin vs. ground conditions
Confirmed correlation with convective heat transfer
Confirmed feasibility of moderate ice reduction

title: Deterministic statistical patterns preceding ice shocks revealed by ice deformation measurements
task: Identifying precursor patterns to ice-shock fracture events
model: (observational analysis) ice deformation measurements with statistical and topological analysis
inputs: Ice deformation time series during springtime thermal expansion
outputs: Statistical precursor signatures, temporal evolution, topological pattern similarity
key_results: Three distinct precursor groups: ~1 hour before, tens of minutes before, and final minutes before ice shock; deterministic patterns emerge preceding failure; increasing topological synchronization observed
comments: Novel statistical framework for fracture precursors in complex media; applicability to other explosive-like processes is promising but requires validation on independent datasets.
hypotheses: Ordered linear features constrain deformation statistics preceding shock
Transition from stochastic to deterministic behavior precedes failure
Multiple groups of short-term precursors exist at different timescales
Topological synchronization of deformation indicates imminent fracture
results: Confirmed emergence of ordered features constraining statistics
Confirmed stochastic-to-deterministic transition
Confirmed three groups of precursors at hour, tens-of-minutes, and minute timescales
Confirmed progressive topological synchronization

title: Étendue and radiance conservation in transformation optics establish strict analytical bounds on field enhancement
task: Establishing fundamental bounds on field enhancement in transformation optics
model: (theory) transformation optics with optical-metric formulation; passive impedance-matched media
inputs: Coordinate transformation; optical phase space, Hamiltonian ray dynamics
outputs: Achievable field enhancement, radiance conservation, phase-space constraints
key_results: Maximum intensity in passive TO concentrator limited solely by geometric area-compression ratio; Liouville's theorem constrains radiance preservation; bounds hold for zero-index, optical-null, and illusion devices
comments: Elegant connection to canonical mechanics and phase-space geometry provides rigorous fundamental bounds, though experimental validation of predicted limits on real metamaterials would be instructive.
hypotheses: Smooth passive impedance-matched TO performs canonical phase-space mapping
Liouville's theorem applies to transformation-optical media
Maximum field enhancement is limited by geometric compression ratio alone
TO redistributes intensity without increasing radiance
results: Confirmed canonical mapping properties of TO
Confirmed Liouville-type phase-space constraint
Confirmed geometric-ratio limitation independent of material
Confirmed intensity redistribution without radiance increase

title: Constructing machine learning interatomic potentials with minimum amount of ab initio data
task: Accelerating machine learning interatomic potential development via model distillation
model: (ML workflow) MACE fine-tuning + NEP distillation with active sampling
inputs: Few hundred additional DFT calculations for target system; pretrained MACE model
outputs: System-specific NEP model with competitive accuracy for large-scale MD
key_results: Single-shot workflow eliminates multiple active-learning loops; fine-tuned MACE inherits pretrained stability; final NEP achieves accuracy competitive with conventional training using ~100× fewer DFT data
comments: Pragmatic reduction in DFT cost for MLIP development is valuable for practical materials discovery, though generalization to diverse material classes and systematic accuracy benchmarking across chemistries would strengthen claims.
hypotheses: Pretrained MACE fine-tuning is more DFT-efficient than start-from-scratch training
Fine-tuned MACE can distill knowledge to smaller NEP model without accuracy loss
MACE-driven sampling strategy eliminates need for iterative active learning
results: Confirmed DFT efficiency of fine-tuning over cold-start
Confirmed successful knowledge distillation from MACE to NEP
Confirmed single-shot workflow feasibility with hundreds of DFT calculations

title: Thermal and dielectric performance of transformer oil-based nanofluid with fullerene C60 nanoparticles
task: Evaluating fullerene C60 nanofluid for transformer oil replacement
model: (experimental + analytical) C60 nanofluid in 250 kVA distribution transformer; natural convection model
inputs: C60 nanoparticle concentration (0.004% w/V); temperature, voltage, oil circulation
outputs: Thermal conductivity, AC breakdown voltage, dissipation factor, temperature rise, flow velocity
key_results: C60 increases AC breakdown voltage by 65% with minimal viscosity/density/conductivity change; slight temperature decrease from enhanced winding-cooling coupling; meets EN 60076-2:2011 standard
comments: Comprehensive industrial validation of nanofluid in real transformer; practical benefit of enhanced breakdown voltage is significant, though flash-point decrease requires monitoring in operational deployment.
hypotheses: C60 nanoparticles enhance dielectric breakdown strength
C60 concentration has minimal impact on viscosity and density
Improved oil circulation enhances cooling efficiency
C60 nanofluid meets industrial transformer standards
results: Confirmed 65% AC breakdown enhancement
Confirmed negligible viscosity/density impacts
Confirmed slight temperature decrease from circulation improvement
Confirmed standard compliance

title: Calcium-mediated calreticulin-IRE1α interaction drives dynamic fluctuation of IRE1α activity under chronic endoplasmic reticulum stress
task: Characterize IRE1α activity dynamics during chronic ER stress
model: (experiment) pharmacologically induced ER stress in cultured cells and mouse liver
inputs: Long-term ER stress induction; calcium concentration changes
outputs: IRE1α phosphorylation levels; calreticulin-IRE1α interaction intensity; ER luminal calcium fluctuation
key_results: IRE1α shows 'up-down-up' fluctuation pattern; calreticulin-IRE1α interaction negatively correlates with calcium concentration; circadian rhythmic oscillation observed in mouse liver
comments: Well-designed study revealing temporal dynamics of a key UPR sensor, though the functional significance of oscillations for cellular outcomes remains partially unclear.
hypotheses: Calcium-mediated calreticulin binding regulates IRE1α activity
Chronic ER stress induces oscillatory rather than sustained IRE1α activation
results: Confirmed: calcium-calreticulin-IRE1α axis controls activity fluctuation
Confirmed: circadian and stress-induced oscillation patterns identified

title: Polar chromosomes are rescued from missegregation by spindle elongation-driven microtubule pivoting
task: Elucidate mechanism rescuing polar chromosomes from missegregation
model: (experiment) live-cell imaging of mitotic spindle dynamics; perturbation of Eg5/KIF11
inputs: Polar chromosome positioning; spindle elongation signals
outputs: Chromosome positioning relative to spindle poles; microtubule pivoting angle and extent; kinetochore attachment status
key_results: Spindle elongation drives microtubule pivoting around centrosome to reposition polar chromosomes; Eg5 activity modulates pivoting direction and extent
comments: Mechanistic insight into a cancer-relevant chromosome segregation error; the contribution of astral vs. kinetochore microtubules is well dissected.
hypotheses: Spindle elongation repositions polar chromosomes via microtubule pivoting
Eg5 activity determines pivoting efficacy
results: Confirmed: spindle elongation necessary for polar chromosome rescue
Confirmed: Eg5 modulates pivoting parameters

title: Phosphoregulation of the novel hemi-arrestin MAPK scaffold Sms1 prevents untimely mating
task: Identify and characterize MAPK scaffold regulating mating pathway in fission yeast
model: (experiment) yeast genetics and cell biology; signal transduction analysis in Schizosaccharomyces pombe
inputs: Pheromone receptor activation; Gα subunit coupling; PIP2 availability
outputs: MAPK cascade assembly; polarized growth; mating response kinetics
key_results: Sms1 hemi-arrestin acts as MAPK scaffold via PIP2 and Gα interactions; phosphorylation-mediated negative feedback prevents untimely mating
comments: Elegant identification of a convergently evolved MAPK scaffold with clear biological output; demonstrates how post-translational modification regulates scaffold function.
hypotheses: Hemi-arrestins function as MAPK scaffolds
Phosphorylation provides negative feedback control of mating signaling
results: Confirmed: Sms1 recruits and organizes MAPK cascade
Confirmed: phosphorylation antagonizes membrane localization and prevents premature mating

title: β-tubulin phosphorylation by Chk1 is required for normal spindle formation during cell division
task: Determine role of Chk1-mediated β-tubulin phosphorylation in spindle formation
model: (experiment) vertebrate cell culture; in vitro kinase assays; centrosome isolation
inputs: Unperturbed mitosis; ATR-ATRIP-TopBP1 pathway activation
outputs: β-tubulin-T285 phosphorylation level; spindle microtubule density and nucleation; chromosome alignment and segregation fidelity
key_results: Chk1 phosphorylates β-tubulin at T285; impaired phosphorylation causes improper spindles, delayed anaphase, and erroneous segregation; ATR-ATRIP-TopBP1 pathway required for Chk1 activation
comments: Unexpected link between DNA damage checkpoint kinase and mitotic spindle assembly in unperturbed mitosis; suggests broader role for checkpoint machinery in normal cell division.
hypotheses: Chk1 phosphorylates β-tubulin at T285
This phosphorylation is necessary for optimal spindle assembly and function
results: Confirmed: β-tubulin T285 phosphorylation by Chk1 identified
Confirmed: phosphorylation required for normal spindle maturation and chromosome segregation

title: Next-generation platelet concentrate: Alb-PRF outperformed PRP and H-PRF in promoting endothelial function and wound healing
task: Compare autologous platelet concentrates for enhancement of endothelial cell function and wound healing
model: (experiment) co-culture of APCs with HUVECs; LPS-induced inflammation model; in vitro assays
inputs: Platelet concentrate type (Alb-PRF, PRP, H-PRF); pro-inflammatory stimulus (LPS)
outputs: Endothelial cell viability, proliferation, migration; CD31 expression; apoptosis resistance
key_results: Alb-PRF superior to PRP and H-PRF in promoting HUVEC proliferation and migration; Alb-PRF rescued LPS-induced apoptosis and enhanced CD31 expression
comments: Practical translational work comparing APC formulations; lacks mechanism of improved Alb-PRF function and requires in vivo wound healing validation.
hypotheses: Alb-PRF formulation enhances endothelial cell function vs. traditional concentrates
Alb-PRF provides cytoprotection under inflammatory stress
results: Confirmed: Alb-PRF outperforms PRP and H-PRF in proliferation and migration assays
Confirmed: Alb-PRF protects against LPS-induced endothelial damage

title: A selective and augmentable butyrate-FFAR2 signal circuitry programs the cellular identity of enteroendocrine L-cells
task: Delineate FFAR2 signaling selectivity in programming enteroendocrine L-cell identity toward PYY secretion
model: (experiment) human L-cell model; mouse intestinal organoids; transcriptomics and secretion assays
inputs: Butyrate (FFAR2 agonist); Notch cascade modulation; biased allosteric FFAR2 agonist (AZ-1729)
outputs: GLP-1 vs. PYY transcript and secretion levels; Notch target expression (Hes1); maturation markers (NeuroD1, Pax6)
key_results: Butyrate drives PYY-biased profile via FFAR2-Gαi signaling without receptor internalization; modulates Notch cascades and late-stage maturation markers; AZ-1729 augments effects
comments: Mechanistically clear demonstration of biased FFAR2 signaling in L-cell programming; translational relevance to metabolic disease control is evident.
hypotheses: Butyrate-FFAR2 signaling preferentially activates PYY over GLP-1 secretion
Biased FFAR2 agonists can pharmacologically amplify this selectivity
results: Confirmed: butyrate induces PYY-biased profile via Gαi axis
Confirmed: AZ-1729 further enhances butyrate-dependent PYY upregulation and Pax6 expression

title: Kynurenine promotes angiogenesis through mTOR signaling in head and neck squamous cell carcinoma
task: Establish kynurenine-mTOR axis in promoting angiogenesis in head and neck squamous cell carcinoma
model: (experiment) TCGA bioinformatic analysis; patient immunohistochemistry; HUVEC culture; CAM and CDX mouse models
inputs: Kynurenine concentration; IDO1 expression level; endothelial cell stimulation
outputs: Endothelial migration rate; tube formation capacity; neovascularization area; mTOR phosphorylation; angiogenesis-related gene expression
key_results: IDO1-driven kynurenine elevated in HNSCC correlates with angiogenesis genes and vascular markers; kynurenine activates mTOR in endothelial cells to upregulate pro-angiogenic factors
comments: Comprehensive multi-level validation from bioinformatics to in vivo models; identifies tryptophan metabolism as targetable pro-angiogenic pathway in HNSCC.
hypotheses: Kynurenine promotes angiogenesis in HNSCC
IDO1-kynurenine-mTOR axis mediates this effect
results: Confirmed: kynurenine enhances endothelial migration, tube formation, and neovascularization
Confirmed: mTOR pathway activation is mechanistic link between kynurenine and angiogenic gene upregulation

title: Androgen receptor localisation and protein interactions provide insight into steroid mediated metabolic shifts in endocrine resistant breast cancer
task: Characterize cytoplasmic androgen receptor localization and protein interactions driving AI-resistant metabolic reprogramming
model: (experiment) breast cancer microarray analysis; LC-MS/MS proteomics; co-immunoprecipitation and confocal imaging; metabolic profiling
inputs: Androgen exposure; aromatase inhibitor resistance status; cellular localization
outputs: Cytoplasmic AR abundance; interacting protein partners; mitochondrial mass and membrane potential; oxidative phosphorylation and glycolysis rates
key_results: High cytoplasmic AR predicts poor survival in luminal B breast cancer; AI-resistant cells show diffuse cytoplasmic AR with elevated mitochondrial function; G3BP1, SLIRP, IGFBP5 identified as metabolic adaptor proteins
comments: Novel link between AR subcellular localization and metabolic rewiring in endocrine resistance; identifies prognostic biomarker and candidate therapeutic targets.
hypotheses: Cytoplasmic AR localizes and interacts with metabolic proteins in AI-resistant cells
These interactions drive oxidative metabolism adaptation
results: Confirmed: cytoplasmic AR abundance correlates with poor prognosis
Confirmed: AR interacts with stress response and metabolic adaptation proteins including G3BP1, SLIRP, IGFBP5

title: Glutamate decarboxylase 1 (GAD1) suppresses the progression of glioblastoma through GSK3β/β-catenin pathway
task: Elucidate GSK3β/β-catenin pathway suppression by GAD1 in glioblastoma progression
model: (experiment) glioblastoma cell lines; expression modulation; pathway inhibition; zebrafish xenotransplantation
inputs: GAD1 expression level; GSK3β phosphorylation status; β-catenin abundance
outputs: Cell proliferation, colony formation, cell cycle phase distribution, migration, invasion rates; Cyclin D1 and MMP9 expression; tumor growth in vivo
key_results: GAD1 downregulation correlates with poor prognosis; GAD1 overexpression suppresses proliferation and invasion via reduced p-GSK3β/β-catenin signaling; GSK3β inhibitor phenocopies GAD1 knockdown effects
comments: Clear mechanistic pathway with in vivo validation; GAD1-GABA link to glioma suppression is novel and potentially exploitable therapeutically.
hypotheses: GAD1 suppresses glioblastoma via GSK3β/β-catenin pathway inhibition
p-GSK3β inactivation controls Cyclin D1 and MMP9 expression
results: Confirmed: GAD1 overexpression reduces p-GSK3β, β-catenin, Cyclin D1, MMP9
Confirmed: GSK3β inhibition phenocopies GAD1 knockdown effects on proliferation and invasion

title: SIRT1 induces FOXO1/autophagy/NCOA4-induced ferroptosis and accelerates the progression of cerebral infarction by inhibiting the E2F1/NOTCH-1/YAP signaling pathway
task: Define SIRT1-mediated ferroptosis pathway in cerebral infarction progression
model: (experiment) rat transient middle cerebral artery occlusion/reperfusion model; astrocyte culture under ischemic stress; Western blotting and flow cytometry
inputs: SIRT1 activation; E2F1/NOTCH-1/YAP signaling status; FOXO1 and autophagy levels
outputs: Infarction area; ferroptosis marker expression (MDA, Fe2+, SOD); autophagy and apoptosis rates; NCOA4-mediated ferroptotic flux
key_results: SIRT1 upregulated in cerebral infarction; SIRT1 activation increases infarction by suppressing E2F1/NOTCH-1/YAP and inducing FOXO1/autophagy/NCOA4-mediated ferroptosis; NOTCH-1 overexpression protective
comments: Complex multi-pathway model linking sirtuins to ferroptosis in stroke; clinical translation requires validation that SIRT1 inhibition reduces infarction.
hypotheses: SIRT1 activation promotes ferroptosis during cerebral infarction
E2F1/NOTCH-1/YAP suppression mediates FOXO1/autophagy/NCOA4-driven ferroptosis
results: Confirmed: SIRT1 activation increases infarction area and ferroptosis markers
Confirmed: SIRT1 suppresses nuclear E2F1/NOTCH-1/YAP while promoting FOXO1, Beclin-2, P62, NCOA4 expression

title: The MIME-seq technique allows to monitor the interaction of small non-coding RNAs with Argonaute proteins and their transfer to other cells
task: Develop MIME-seq2.0 for tracking small RNA-Argonaute interactions and intercellular transfer
model: (experiment) engineered methyltransferase-Argonaute binding chimera; MIN6B1 insulin-secreting cells, Jurkat T cells, C2C12 myotubes; RNA-seq and qPCR
inputs: Small non-coding RNAs; Argonaute protein binding; extracellular vesicle release
outputs: Oxidation-protected sncRNA profile; miRNA-Ago2 binding status; transferred miRNA detection in recipient cells
key_results: MIME-seq2.0 efficiently distinguishes oxidized and non-oxidized sncRNAs; protects miRNAs, Y-RNAs, and tRNA fragments; enables sensitive detection of EV-transferred miRNAs between cell types
comments: Methodological advance with clear technical merit; enables previously inaccessible measurements of RNA oxidation status and intercellular RNA transfer dynamics.
hypotheses: MIME enzyme protects sncRNAs from oxidation to enable cloning
Protected miRNAs can be tracked across cell boundaries via EVs
results: Confirmed: MIME enzyme protects miRNAs, Y-RNAs, and tRNA fragments from oxidation
Confirmed: transferred miRNAs in EVs protected and detectably accumulated in recipient cells

title: Hydroxychloroquine alleviates cyclophosphamide-induced premature ovarian failure by attenuating granulosa cell senescence and modulating the mtDNA-cGAS pathway
task: Determine hydroxychloroquine protection against cyclophosphamide-induced premature ovarian failure
model: (experiment) CTX-induced POF mouse model; human granulosa cell line (KGN); phosphoramide mustard (PM) exposure; mitochondrial and STING pathway analysis
inputs: Cyclophosphamide or PM exposure; hydroxychloroquine treatment; mtDNA leakage status
outputs: Ovarian function markers; follicle count; hormone levels; granulosa cell senescence; ROS and mtDNA levels; cGAS-STING pathway activation
key_results: HCQ reverses CTX-induced ovarian dysfunction and reduces granulosa cell senescence; decreases mtDNA leakage and ROS; inhibits cGAS-STING pathway and SASP expression
comments: Mechanistically sound preclinical study with clear translational path for fertility preservation in cancer patients; demonstrates mtDNA-cGAS axis as druggable target.
hypotheses: HCQ protects against CTX-induced premature ovarian failure
mtDNA leakage triggers cGAS-STING-dependent senescence and SASP
results: Confirmed: HCQ improves ovarian function and reduces follicular depletion
Confirmed: HCQ suppresses mtDNA leakage, ROS production, and cGAS-STING pathway activation

title: Systematic background selection with BasCoD enhances contrastive dimension reduction in single cell genomics
task: Develop BasCoD framework for systematic background selection in contrastive dimension reduction
model: (theory) spectral subspace inclusion theory; contrastive learning; statistical hypothesis testing
inputs: Target and background single-cell datasets; heterogeneous experimental conditions
outputs: Background suitability scores; recommended background selections; improved target representation contrast
key_results: BasCoD identifies suitable backgrounds that enhance contrast and interpretability of target representations; guides design of contrastive analyses in large-scale single-cell experiments
comments: Valuable methodological framework addressing practical challenge in single-cell genomics; enables principled background selection rather than ad hoc choices.
hypotheses: Spectral subspace inclusion theory enables rigorous background selection evaluation
Appropriate background selection substantially improves contrastive dimension reduction
results: Confirmed: BasCoD identifies suitable backgrounds across diverse datasets
Confirmed: appropriate backgrounds enhance contrast and biological interpretability of representations

title: Molecular basis of oocyte cytoplasmic lattice assembly
task: Determine molecular architecture and assembly mechanism of oocyte cytoplasmic lattice
model: (experiment) cryo-electron microscopy of isolated mouse oocyte cytoplasmic lattice; structural analysis and modeling
inputs: Cytoplasmic lattice protein components; assembly forces and interactions
outputs: CPL three-dimensional structure; constituent protein identification; repeating unit architecture; interaction network topology
key_results: CPL comprises 14 protein subunits assembled into repeating 'U-shaped basket' (UB) and 'adapter ring' (AR) units; PADI6 didecamer forms UB core; AR connects adjacent UBs via SCMC dimers; multiple interaction clusters maintain filamentous architecture
comments: Exceptional structural biology work providing atomic-level understanding of an essential embryonic matrix; reveals principles of large periodic protein lattice assembly.
hypotheses: CPL forms repeating modular units
Multiple interaction clusters maintain structural periodicity
results: Confirmed: CPL consists of UB-AR repeating units with specific protein composition
Confirmed: PADI6 pentamers and SCMC dimer networks form structural core

title: BTG3 suppresses oral squamous cell carcinoma progression by inhibiting PI3K/AKT signaling and EMT
task: Characterize BTG3 tumor suppressor function in oral squamous cell carcinoma via PI3K/AKT pathway
model: (experiment) OSCC cell lines (CAL27, SCC15) and tissues; expression modulation; pathway analysis; cell cycle and migration assays
inputs: BTG3 expression level; PI3K/AKT phosphorylation status; EMT marker expression
outputs: Cell proliferation rate; migration and invasion capacity; cell cycle phase distribution; PI3K/AKT signaling output; E-cadherin and mesenchymal marker levels
key_results: BTG3 downregulated in OSCC correlates with poor prognosis; BTG3 overexpression suppresses proliferation, migration, invasion, and G1 cell cycle arrest via PI3K/AKT inhibition and EMT suppression
comments: Establishes BTG3 as tumor suppressor in OSCC with clear mechanistic pathway; pathway inhibition appears to be primary mode of action.
hypotheses: BTG3 suppresses OSCC via PI3K/AKT pathway inhibition
BTG3-mediated AKT inhibition blocks EMT progression
results: Confirmed: BTG3 overexpression inhibits proliferation, migration, invasion
Confirmed: BTG3 overexpression suppresses PI3K/AKT signaling and EMT markers
